---
title: "Alpha Diversity"
author: "Nitin Bayal"
format: html
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Introduction

This document reports on a microbiota project results of alpha diversity across different diets and visits.
```{r variable}
 #STEP1: alpha diversity
# Define variables
variable <- "group"  
```

**Group variable: `r variable`**
```{r data-load}
# tse <- readRDS("../data/tse.Rds")
source("data.R")
```

## Data Summary
```{r data-summary}
tse
```

## Richness: Observed species index
```{r observed-species}

p <- plotColData(
    tse,
    "observed",
    variable,
    colour_by = variable, show_boxplot = TRUE, show_violin = FALSE) +
    theme(text = element_text(size = 10)) +
    labs(x = "Groups", y = expression(Richness [Observed]))
p
```

## Group-wise comparisons: Shannon index
```{r shannon-index}
index <- "shannon"

# Calculate p-values
pvals <- pairwise.wilcox.test(
    tse[[index]], tse[[variable]], p.adjust.method = "fdr")
# Put them to data.frame format
pvals <- pvals[["p.value"]] |>
    as.data.frame()
varname <- "group1"
pvals[[varname]] <- rownames(pvals)
# To long format
pvals <- reshape(
    pvals,
    direction = "long",
    varying = colnames(pvals)[ !colnames(pvals) %in% varname ],
    times = colnames(pvals)[ !colnames(pvals) %in% varname ],
    v.names = "p",
    timevar = "group2",
    idvar = "group1"
    ) |>
    na.omit()
# Add y-axis position
pvals[["y.position"]] <- apply(pvals, 1, function(x){
    temp1 <- tse[[index]][ tse[[variable]] == x[["group1"]] ]
    temp2 <- tse[[index]][ tse[[variable]] == x[["group2"]] ]
    temp <- max( c(temp1, temp2) )
    return(temp)
})
pvals[["y.position"]] <- max(pvals[["y.position"]]) +
    order(pvals[["y.position"]]) * 0.2
# Round values
pvals[["p"]] <- round(pvals[["p"]], 3)

# Create a boxplot
p <- plotColData(
    tse, x = variable, y = index,
    show_boxplot = TRUE, show_violin = FALSE, colour_by = variable) +
    theme(text = element_text(size = 10)) +
    stat_pvalue_manual(pvals)
p
```

## Table of the top findings
```{r table-of-findings}
final_results <- run_diversity_tests(tse, comparisons, variable, indices)
datatable(final_results, 
        options = list(pageLength = 10), 
        caption = paste("Table of the top findings"))
```
